summary: Backfill landmark agent papers missed by D1's strict filters. Relaxed citation thresholds for recent works; explicit OpenAlex lookups for canonical agent papers (ReAct, Toolformer, Reflexion, AutoGen, MetaGPT, Generative Agents, SWE-bench, etc.).

notes_on_landmarks.reason: OpenAlex W4304195432 record is the ReAct arxiv version (2022), and the only year-2023 indexed record under that title is a misattribution. Hard year filter year>=2023 excludes the arxiv version. Captured downstream effect via Reflexion, Toolformer, HuggingGPT which all build on ReAct.

OpenAlex record W4385474529 has a malformed display title (shows 'Counterfactually Auditable Lifecycle Certification') but the underlying DOI 10.48550/arxiv.2307.16789, authors list (Qin Yujia, Shihao Liang, Yining Ye, ...), abstract, and arxiv ID all confirm it is the ToolLLM paper. Title manually corrected in papers.json.
Earliest indexed record on OpenAlex has publication_year=2022 (arXiv 2203.11171). STRICT year filter rejects.
No peer-reviewed eval paper for BabyAGI on OpenAlex meeting threshold. Captured via 'Auto-GPT for Online Decision Making' (Yang et al. 2023) as AGT-045.
No qualifying OpenAlex publication; tooling reports rather than research papers.

OpenAlex did not surface a qualifying record in title.search; landmark task instructions noted Mind2Web (already AGT-013 in D1) plus VisualWebArena (added as AGT-041) cover the web-agent slate. Will rely on D3 to score Mind2Web for this claim.

filter_relaxations.citation_thresholds: For 2023: >=200 preferred, but landmark papers included down to ~30 cites per task instruction 'prefer including borderline'.
filter_relaxations.venue: arXiv preprints accepted; AI venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, ACL, EMNLP, AAAI) accepted.
